message:
Add jfp notes command for managing personal prompt annotations

Implements jfp notes <prompt-id> command with:
- List notes: jfp notes <prompt-id>
- Add note: jfp notes <prompt-id> --add 'text'
- Delete note: jfp notes <prompt-id> --delete <note-id>
- --json flag for machine-readable output

Features:
- Requires authentication (prompts login if not authenticated)
- Validates prompt exists before API call
- Proper error handling for auth errors, premium requirements
- Clean human-readable and JSON output modes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","title":"Implement jfp save <prompt-id> command","description":"Implement jfp save command to save prompts to user's premium account.

Usage: jfp save <prompt-id>

Features:
- Requires premium authentication (prompts user to login if not authenticated)
- Validates prompt ID exists in local registry before saving
- POST to /api/cli/saved-prompts with { prompt_id }
- Displays success message with saved prompt title
- Handles duplicate saves gracefully (already saved message)
- Requires premium tier (show upgrade message for free users)

Error Handling:
- 401: Prompt to login
- 403: Display "Saving prompts requires premium subscription"
- 404: Display "Prompt not found"
- Network errors: Display connection error message

Output Format:
- Default: "Saved: [prompt title]"
- With --json: { saved: true, prompt_id, title, saved_at }

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output.

Commands to Update:
- jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier })
- jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true })
- jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id })
- jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] })
- jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] })
- jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] })
- jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id })
- jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] })

Implementation:
- Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands
- When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout
- Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code }
- Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled
- Ensure consistent field naming across all commands

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-c8l","title":"Implement tokenizer (search/tokenize.ts)","description":"**Details:**
- `tokenize(text)` splits text into searchable tokens
- Lowercase normalization
- Punctuation removal (preserve hyphens in compound words)
- Stopword filtering (the, a, an, is, are, etc.)
- Optional stemming (simple suffix stripping)
- Unicode normalization (NFD→NFC)

**Implementation:**
```typescript
export function tokenize(text: string): string[] {
  return text
    .toLowerCase()
    .normalize('NFD')
    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, ' ')
    .split(/\s+/)
    .filter(token => token.length > 1 && !STOPWORDS.has(token));
}
```

**Stopwords:** Common English stopwords (50-100 words)

**Used By:** BM25 search, synonym expansion

**Reasoning:** Consistent tokenization is foundational for search quality. Must be fast (run on every keystroke in SpotlightSearch) and deterministic.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:32:51.034344375-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:07.171689686-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:07.171689686-05:00","close_reason":"Core package fully implemented with types, registry, bundles, workflows, schema, search (tokenize, synonyms, BM25, engine), export (skills, markdown, json), and template (render, variables)"}
